frameworks.name: neural decoding
frameworks.one_line: reading out distributed neural representations to interpret encoded information

frameworks.name: mechanistic readout models
frameworks.one_line: models of how downstream regions actually read upstream representations

frameworks.name: encoding-decoding framework
frameworks.one_line: brain as series of encode-decode computations supporting adaptive behavior

core_question: How should neural decoding analyses be combined with mechanistic models of downstream readout to explain adaptive behavior?
key_assumptions: neurons act in concert to produce perception, cognition, and behavior
brain areas are specialized to varying extents
distributed circuits continuously encode environmental and body-state features
downstream areas decode these representations for decisions and movement
interpretations: decoding analyses alone are insufficient and must be complemented with mechanistic readout models
the distributed brain can be conceptualized as serial encode-decode computations for adaptive behavior
